normalization: Generated GitGraph commit ids are compared by replay sequence, preserving Agentic Mermaid deterministic c<N> identity as a documented divergence.
GitGraph numeric upstream commit types are compared by semantic names NORMAL, REVERSE, HIGHLIGHT, MERGE, and CHERRY_PICK.
Mindmap expectations are flattened to id, label, shape, parent, icon, and class fields; indentation remains verbatim in each source.

        branch branch01

        branch cherry-pick03
        branch branch/example-branch
        branch merge/test_merge
        %% single character branch name
        branch A

upstream.block: should handle merge with custom ids, tags and type

blocks.source:
gitGraph:
          commit
          branch testBranch
          checkout testBranch
          commit
          checkout main
          %% Merge Tag and ID
          merge testBranch tag: "merge-tag" id: "2-222"
          branch testBranch2
          checkout testBranch2
          commit
          checkout main
          %% Merge ID and Tag (reverse order)
          merge testBranch2 id: "4-444" tag: "merge-tag2" type:HIGHLIGHT
          branch testBranch3
          checkout testBranch3
          commit
          checkout main
          %% just Merge ID
          merge testBranch3 id: "6-666"

This is a description
using multiple lines

upstream.block: `should work with custom commit id or branch name ${prop}`

variants.source:
gitGraph
        commit id:"__proto__"
        branch __proto__
        checkout __proto__
        commit
        checkout main
        merge __proto__

gitGraph
        commit id:"constructor"
        branch constructor
        checkout constructor
        commit
        checkout main
        merge constructor

upstream.block: should log a warning when two commits have the same ID

blocks.summary: Upstream logs and keeps replaying after a duplicate commit id; Agentic Mermaid rejects ambiguous identity deterministically.
blocks.source:
gitGraph
     commit id:"initial commit"
     commit id:"work on first release"
     commit id:"design freeze from here"
     branch v1-rc
     checkout v1-rc
     commit id:"bugfix 1"
     commit id:"bigfix 2" tag:"v1.0.1"
     branch FORK-v1.0-MDR
     checkout FORK-v1.0-MDR
     commit id:"working on MDR"
     checkout v1-rc
     commit id:"minor design changes for MDR" tag:"v1.0.2"
     checkout FORK-v1.0-MDR
     merge v1-rc
     checkout main
     commit id:"new feature for v1.1…"
     checkout FORK-v1.0-MDR
     commit id:"working on MDR"
     commit id:"finishing MDR"
     branch v1.0-MDR
     checkout v1.0-MDR
     commit id:"brush up release" tag:"v1.0.2-MDR"
     checkout v1-rc
     commit id:"bugfix without MDR"
     checkout main
     commit id:"work on v1.1"

intentionalDivergences.upstream: Generates random commit ids when id: is omitted.
intentionalDivergences.local: Generates deterministic c<N> ids in replay order.
intentionalDivergences.reason: Identical input must produce identical semantic identity and SVG bytes.

intentionalDivergences.upstream: May retain parser-generated node bookkeeping without a public duplicate-id diagnostic.
intentionalDivergences.local: Rejects duplicate semantic ids with MINDMAP_DUPLICATE_ID.
intentionalDivergences.reason: Typed mutation and SVG data-id require an unambiguous source identity.

intentionalDivergences.upstream: Logs a warning and continues after a duplicate custom id.
intentionalDivergences.local: Rejects the duplicate with GITGRAPH_DUPLICATE_COMMIT_ID before rendering.
intentionalDivergences.reason: History replay must never silently overwrite an existing commit.
